title: CIL Geology — Full-length Mock 09
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who discovered the electron?
questions.solution: J.J. Thomson

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Speed of boat in still water is 10 km/h. If it takes 6 h to go 36 km upstream, stream speed:
questions.solution: 4 km/h

questions.solution: 7.5 cm

questions.solution: Standard deviation

questions.stem: If x = 3 + √8, then x² + 1/x² is:

questions.stem: Probability that a card from 52 is an ace of spades:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If LCM of three numbers is 7920 and HCF is 12, which of these CANNOT be one of the numbers?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If a train runs at 40 km/h, it reaches 15 min late. At 50 km/h, 5 min late. Distance:
questions.solution: 50 km

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability

questions.solution: 14.29 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat goes 24 km upstream and 28 km downstream in 8 h. 30 km upstream and 21 km downstream in 9 h. Speed of stream:
questions.solution: 4 km/h

questions.stem: A trader buys 20 articles for ₹1,000 and sells 15 for ₹1,200. Profit percent:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A milkman adds water to milk and sells at cost price, gaining 16.67%. Water in mixture:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: The new AI system can detect roof falls in mines 30 seconds before they occur. Conclusion I: Mine safety will significantly improve. II: Human monitoring will become completely redundant.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A committee has 4 members with 2 boys and 2 girls. Ravi and Simran cannot be together. In how many ways can they be selected from 5 boys and 5 girls if Ravi and Simran are among them?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A starts from point S, goes 10 m north to T. Then 24 m east to U. Distance S to U:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 15 March 2026 is Sunday, what day is 15 August 2026?
questions.solution: Saturday

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: 7 students sit in a row. R is 4th from left. S is 3rd from right. T is left of R. U is between R and S. How many between T and S?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a class of 40, Ravi is 7th from top. How many are below him?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: In CIL, 60% of employees are below 35 years. Conclusion I: CIL has a young workforce. II: Most CIL employees are experienced.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A school has 3 sections. Each student must choose at least 2 of 4 languages. Minimum number of language choices per student:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Ananya faces east. She turns 180° clockwise, then 90° anticlockwise, then 45° clockwise. Now facing:
questions.solution: South-west

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Angle between hour and minute hand at 10:10:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A,B,C,D,E,F sit in a circle. A is between B and C. D is opposite B. E is 2nd left of D. Who is opposite A?
questions.solution: E

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a queue, Shyam is 12th from front and 18th from back. Total people:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: New mining technology reduces dust emissions by 60%. Conclusion I: The technology will be adopted by all mines. II: Dust pollution from mines will be eliminated.
questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: An examination has 6 subjects. A student must pass at least 4. How many combinations of passing subjects?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: From point X, A is 40 m north. B is 30 m east of A. C is 40 m south of B. X is in which direction of C?
questions.solution: West

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Angle between hour and minute hand at 6:20:

questions.stem: Five girls sit in a row. Pooja is left of Ritu but right of Sana. Tina is right of Ritu. Uma is left of Sana. Who is in middle?
questions.solution: Pooja

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Count of right angles in shapes: 0, 4, 1, 4, ? (alternating)

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Eight people in square arrangement: P, Q, R, S corners; T, U, V, W edges. P opposite R. Q adjacent to P. T between P and Q. U opposite T. Who is between Q and R?
questions.solution: V

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 1234 = ENER, 5678 = STIC, then 1357 = ?
questions.solution: ENST

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Chord : Circle :: Diameter : ?
questions.solution: Circle

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Angle between minute hand and hour hand at 7:40:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a code, 512 means 'you are good', 231 means 'they are bad', 145 means 'good and bad'. Code for 'they' is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A is B's sister. C is B's mother. D is C's father. How is A related to D?
questions.solution: Granddaughter

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In code, MINING = 84, COAL = 38, GEOLOGY = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Democracy is a system of government by the people. Q: Citizens elect their representatives through free and fair elections. R: These representatives make laws on behalf of the people. S: An independent judiciary ensures that laws are followed.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PRUDENT:
questions.solution: Cautious

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Earth's atmosphere is composed of several layers. Q: The troposphere is the lowest layer where weather occurs. R: Above it lies the stratosphere containing the ozone layer. S: The mesosphere and thermosphere extend further upward.
questions.solution: PRQS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of ABUNDANT:
questions.solution: Plentiful

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Climate change is one of the biggest challenges today. Q: Rising temperatures cause extreme weather events. R: Governments worldwide are taking mitigation measures. S: However, collective action at individual level is also needed.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of BENEVOLENT:
questions.solution: Charitable

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Cyber security is a growing concern for organisations. Q: Data breaches can result in significant financial losses. R: Companies invest in firewalls and encryption technologies. S: Employee training is essential to prevent phishing attacks.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of INTREPID:
questions.solution: Fearless

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Artificial intelligence is transforming industries. Q: Machine learning algorithms analyse vast amounts of data. R: AI-powered systems can make predictions and recommendations. S: Ethical considerations around AI are being debated.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of RIGOROUS:
questions.solution: Thorough

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Renewable energy sources are gaining importance. Q: Solar and wind power are the most promising. R: Their cost has decreased dramatically. S: Many countries have set ambitious renewable energy targets.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of ARDENT:
questions.solution: Enthusiastic

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Industrial Revolution transformed manufacturing processes. Q: It began in England in the late 18th century. R: Steam engines powered factories and railways. S: This led to unprecedented economic growth.
questions.solution: QPRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of CONUNDRUM:
questions.solution: Puzzle

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Sustainable development balances economic growth and environmental protection. Q: The concept gained global attention after the Brundtland Report. R: It emphasises meeting present needs without compromising future generations. S: The UN Sustainable Development Goals outline specific targets.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of TENACIOUS:
questions.solution: Determined

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: It is the second largest producer of coal in the world. Q: India has vast mineral resources. R: It also has significant reserves of iron ore and bauxite. S: The mining sector contributes substantially to the economy.
questions.solution: QRPS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To cost an arm and a leg':
questions.solution: To be very expensive

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ABUNDANT:
questions.solution: Scarce

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Open-pit mining is used when coal seams are near the surface. Q: The topsoil and overburden are removed first. R: The coal is then extracted using heavy machinery. S: After mining, the land is reclaimed for other uses.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She was angry on her friend for lying.
options: She was angry to her friend for lying.
She was angry on her friend for lying.
She was angry with her friend for lying.
She was angry at her friend for lying.
questions.solution: She was angry with her friend for lying.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The workers have been ____ for a pay rise.
questions.solution: agitating

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
options: Neither he nor his brother were going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother have been going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.
questions.solution: Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The manager ____ the proposal before the board.
questions.solution: placed

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of VENERATE:
questions.solution: Revere

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Triassic-Jurassic extinction (~201 Ma):
options: 76% species lost. CAMP volcanism (Central Atlantic Magmatic Province). Pangea breakup
questions.solution: Tr-J: allowed dinosaur dominance. CAMP: huge basalt flows. CO2 → warming → ocean acidification.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Peneplain represents:
questions.solution: Peneplain: nearly flat surface near base level. End-cycle landscape of Davisian cycle. Almost at sea level.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Landsat 8 OLI bands: Band 4 (Red), Band 5 (NIR), Band 6 (SWIR1). NDVI formula:
questions.solution: NDVI: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index. Healthy vegetation: high NDVI (0.6-0.9).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Parasequence stacking patterns:
options: Progradational (coarsening up, shallowing), Retrogradational (fining up, deepening), Aggradational (same facies)
questions.solution: Parasequence: shallowing-upward cycle. Progradational: Parasequence sets thicken upward.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness of calcite:
questions.solution: Calcite (CaCO3) — hardness 3. Scratched by copper coin. Effervesces in HCl.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Index minerals such as chlorite → biotite → garnet → kyanite → sillimanite mark increasing:
questions.solution: These index minerals appear at successive isograds, recording rising metamorphic grade.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inertinite macerals origin:
options: Oxidized or charred plant material (fusain, charcoal)
questions.solution: Inertinite: fusinite, semifusinite, micrinite. High carbon, low volatile. May be wildfire products.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetic striping at mid-ocean ridges:
options: Symmetric pattern of normal/reversed polarity. Evidence for seafloor spreading
questions.solution: Vine-Matthews (1963): magnetic anomalies symmetric about ridge. Records geomagnetic reversals.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Wilson cycle describes:
options: Opening and closing of ocean basins over geological time
questions.solution: Wilson: rift to ocean to subduction to collision to orogeny.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Index fossil criteria: short range, wide distribution, abundant, easily recognizable
options: Short range, wide distribution, abundant, easily recognizable
questions.solution: Index fossils: guide fossils. Used for biostratigraphic correlation. E.g., graptolites, ammonites, trilobites.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Dinosaur extinction at K-Pg boundary:
questions.solution: K-Pg: asteroid impact (iridium anomaly, shocked quartz) + Deccan Traps. Ended Mesozoic Era.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mineral processing: froth flotation reagents:
questions.solution: Collector: renders mineral hydrophobic. Frother: stabilizes bubbles. Depressant: prevents flotation.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Carbon isotope δ13C excursion indicates:
questions.solution: δ13C: fractionation by photosynthesis. Excursions indicate mass extinctions, methane release, or organic carbon burial.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Skarn:
options: Ca-Fe-Mg silicate rock. Contact metamorphic/metasomatic. Ore deposits (W, Cu, Fe)
questions.solution: Skarn: carbonate rock altered by intrusion. Pyroxene, garnet, wollastonite. Tactite.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Glomeroporphyritic texture indicates:
questions.solution: Glomeroporphyritic: clusters of phenocrysts. Crystal mush accumulation, or synneusis (clumping in melt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetostratigraphy: normal polarity = same as present field. Reversed:
options: Magnetic minerals aligned opposite to present field direction
questions.solution: Geomagnetic polarity time scale (GPTS). Cande and Kent (1995). Chron boundaries.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Wave-cut platform is formed by:
options: Wave erosion at base of cliff
questions.solution: Wave-cut platform: horizontal rock surface at base of sea cliff, formed by wave abrasion. Retreating cliff leaves platform.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rock mass classification — RMR:
options: Bieniawski. UCS + RQD + joint spacing + condition + groundwater
questions.solution: RMR: sum of 5 parameters. Class I (81-100, very good) to V (<20, very poor).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shear sense indicators — sigma (σ) porphyroclast:
options: Tails at angle to foliation. σ_a (asymmetric tails on both sides) and σ_c (tails in pressure shadow)
questions.solution: σ: tails parallel to shear direction. σ-type: tails merge in matrix. δ-type: tails embayed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sea-floor spreading:
options: Mid-ocean ridges create new oceanic crust. Magnetic striping (Vine-Matthews). Age increasing from ridge
questions.solution: Spreading rate: slow (1-2 cm/yr, Mid-Atlantic), fast (5-10 cm/yr, East Pacific Rise).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ocean island basalt (OIB) — plume:
options: Enriched mantle (primitive + subducted). (La/Sm)_N > 1. Garnet in source → high Sm/Yb
questions.solution: OIB: Hawaii, Iceland. Pb-isotope: HIMU, EM1, EM2 mantle components. High ³He/⁴He.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aquifuge:
questions.solution: Aquifuge: no primary porosity, no secondary porosity. No water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Miller-Bravais indices (hkil) for hexagonal system:
questions.solution: 4-index: h, k, i=-(h+k), l. {10-10} = prism face. {0001} = basal pinacoid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Greenschist facies: typical minerals:
questions.solution: Greenschist: 300-500C, low-medium P. Greenschist facies: green minerals. Basalt composition.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Drumlin:
options: Streamlined glacial till hill. Stoss (steep) end up-ice, tail down-ice
questions.solution: Drumlin: ice flow direction indicator. Swarm: 'basket of eggs' topography.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A dipping coal seam outcrops over a horizontal width of 100 m on flat ground and dips at 50°. Its true thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = horizontal width × sin(dip) = 100×sin50° ≈ 76.6 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Moho discontinuity depth:
options: ~5-10 km (oceanic), ~30-50 km (continental). Base of crust. Seismic P-wave jump ~6.7→8.1 km/s
questions.solution: Moho: crust-mantle boundary. Defined by Vp. Faster in mafic lower crust.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vertical Electrical Sounding (VES):
options: Current electrode spacing increased → probes deeper. 1D layered earth model
questions.solution: VES: Wenner or Schlumberger. AB/2 increases → deeper. Curve matching or inversion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flood basalt eruptions:
options: Large igneous provinces (LIPs). Deccan, Siberian Traps. Columnar jointing
questions.solution: Flood basalt: fissure eruptions. 10³-10⁶ km². Some linked to mass extinctions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Birefringence:
options: Difference between highest and lowest refractive index
questions.solution: Birefringence = nγ - nα. Calcite: 0.172 (very high). Quartz: 0.009 (low).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Joints:
options: Fractures with no displacement. Systematic (parallel) vs non-systematic
questions.solution: Joints: cooling (columnar), tectonic (shear, extension), unloading (exfoliation).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mantle minerals: ringwoodite in transition zone (410-660 km):
options: Spinel structure, (Mg,Fe)₂SiO₄. High-pressure polymorph of olivine
questions.solution: Ringwoodite: spinel structure. 520 km discontinuity. Ringwoodite inclusions in diamond: water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which property makes a fossil a good index fossil?
options: Short geological range plus wide geographic distribution
questions.solution: Brief existence pins a narrow time; wide spread allows correlation across regions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electron Spin Resonance (ESR):
options: Trapped electrons in tooth enamel, calcite, quartz. Range: few ka to ~2 Ma. Non-destructive
questions.solution: ESR: detects unpaired electrons. Used for fault gouge dating. Combined ESR + U-series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fission track annealing in apatite:
options: Tracks anneal at ~60-120°C. Partial annealing zone (PAZ): 60-120°C
questions.solution: Apatite FTT: thermochronometer. Tracks stable <60°C. Fully annealed >120°C. Track length analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ar-Ar dating advantage over K-Ar:
options: Single heating step or step heating for age spectrum. No need for separate K measurement
questions.solution: Ar-Ar: irradiation converts ³⁹K to ³⁹Ar. Step heating: age spectrum. Plateau age = undisturbed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: After 4 half-lives, the percentage of the original radioactive parent isotope remaining is:
questions.solution: Fraction remaining = (1/2)^4 = 6.25%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: C-14 dating limitations:
options: Only up to ~50,000 years. Requires organic material. Calibration needed
questions.solution: C-14: T½=5730 years. Effective ~50ka (8 half-lives). Calibration: IntCal20. AMS for small samples.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A normal fault is produced mainly by:
questions.solution: Tension drops the hanging wall down relative to the footwall — a normal fault.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The progressive transformation of plant matter into higher-rank coal is called:
questions.solution: Coalification increases carbon and reduces moisture/volatiles as burial heat and pressure rise.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 25 m/day) of cross-section 500 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness of talc on Mohs scale:
questions.solution: Talc (Mg3Si4O10(OH)2) — softest mineral. Diamond = 10.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Marine terrace correlation:
options: Elevated wave-cut platforms. Correlated with sea-level highstands (MIS stages)
questions.solution: Terraces: marine isotope stages. MIS 5e (125ka) is well preserved. Tectonic uplift rate calculation.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: DEM resolution of 30m means:
questions.solution: DEM: elevation values in grid. 30m = horizontal resolution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rock Quality Designation (RQD):
questions.solution: RQD: sum of pieces >100mm/total core length × 100. Rock mass classification.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fractional crystallization:
options: Early-formed crystals removed from melt, changing magma composition
questions.solution: Bowen's series: early olivine settles, residual melt becomes more felsic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cambrian explosion:
options: Rapid appearance of major animal phyla (~541 Ma). Burgess Shale (507 Ma)
questions.solution: Burgess Shale (Canadian Rockies): exceptional preservation (soft parts). Trilobites, brachiopods.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pelagic sediments:
options: Biogenic ooze (calcareous/siliceous) and red clay. Slow accumulation
questions.solution: Calcareous: foraminifera, coccoliths. Siliceous: radiolaria, diatoms. CCD controls carbonate preservation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eclogite:
options: Very high P. Garnet + omphacite (sodic pyroxene). Density ~3.5 g/cm³
questions.solution: Eclogite: basaltic composition at >12 kbar. Density similar to mantle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Well logging — neutron (CNL):
options: Hydrogen index → porosity. Water-filled pores. Log overlay for gas (gas crossover with density)
questions.solution: Neutron: Am-Be source. Thermal neutron capture. Shale: high neutron porosity (bound water).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mineral exploration: geophysical methods for base metal exploration:
options: EM (electromagnetic) methods detect conductive massive sulfides
questions.solution: EM: time-domain or frequency-domain. Massive sulfides: good conductors. Detectable at depth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 50% of its original parent isotope (half-life 4.47 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 50% remaining ⇒ 1 half-lives ⇒ age = 1×4.47 = 4.47 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dip-slip fault:
options: Movement along dip direction (normal or reverse)
questions.solution: Dip-slip: vertical component. Oblique-slip: both dip and strike components.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An opencast block of 50000 m² has 50 m of overburden above the seam. The overburden volume to be removed is:
questions.solution: Volume = area × depth = 50000×50 = 2500000 m³ = 2.5 million m³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Evaporation measurement — pan:
options: Class A evaporation pan. Pan coefficient (0.7-0.8) to convert to lake evaporation
questions.solution: Class A pan: 1.2m diameter, 0.25m deep. Pan evaporation > lake evaporation (K_p).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cleavage in calcite:
questions.solution: Calcite (CaCO3) — double refraction. Effervesces in dilute HCl.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Plate tectonics — types:
questions.solution: Tectonic plates: 7 major (Pacific, N.Am, S.Am, Africa, Eurasia, Australia, Antarctica). Move cm/yr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) in remote sensing:
options: Reduces correlated bands to uncorrelated components. PC1 = maximum variance
questions.solution: PCA: dimensionality reduction. PC1: brightness. PC2: contrast. PC3: noise usually.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Borehole geophysics:
options: Gamma, resistivity, SP, caliper, sonic logging. In-situ rock properties
questions.solution: Well logging: lithology, porosity, density, water quality. Acoustic televiewer: fractures.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ophiolite suite:
options: Oceanic crust sequence: peridotite → gabbro → sheeted dykes → pillow basalt → chert
questions.solution: Ophiolite: obducted oceanic lithosphere. Troodos (Cyprus), Oman.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geological time scale divisions (largest to smallest):
questions.solution: Eon: Precambrian, Phanerozoic. Era: Paleozoic, Mesozoic, Cenozoic. Period: Cambrian, etc.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porosity in sedimentary rocks:
questions.solution: Sandstone: intergranular porosity. Carbonates: vuggy, moldic, fracture porosity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Well log correlation: gamma ray log identifies:
options: Shale content (high GR = shale, low GR = sand/carbonate)
questions.solution: GR: natural radioactivity. K, U, Th. Shale: high GR. Sand: low GR. Clean carbonate: low GR.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A dipping coal seam outcrops over a horizontal width of 50 m on flat ground and dips at 50°. Its true thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = horizontal width × sin(dip) = 50×sin50° ≈ 38.3 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aquifer pumping test — Jacob method:
questions.solution: Jacob: t₀ from s=0 intercept. T = 2.303Q/(4π·Δs). S = 2.25T·t₀/r².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Floodplain features:
options: Natural levee, backswamp, oxbow lake, yazoo stream, crevasse splay
questions.solution: Floodplain: flat area adjacent to river, periodically flooded. Alluvial soil.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stock:
questions.solution: Stock: similar to batholith but smaller. Often associated with mineral deposits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic grade sequence (pelitic):
questions.solution: Increasing T/P. Slate (low), phyllite (sheen), schist (crystals visible), gneiss (bands).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Evapotranspiration:
options: Combined evaporation + transpiration. Reference ET (ETo) from weather data
questions.solution: Penman-Monteith: ETo = (0.408Δ(R_n-G)+γ·900/(T+273)u₂(e_s-e_a))/(Δ+γ(1+0.34u₂)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stone columns:
options: Vibro-replacement. Granular columns improve bearing capacity and drainage
questions.solution: Stone columns: 0.6-1.1m diameter. Install by vibroflot. Reduce liquefaction. Increase BC 2-3×.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Arranged from lowest to highest rank, the coal series is:
questions.solution: Increasing rank (carbon content) goes peat, lignite, sub-bituminous/bituminous, anthracite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geophysical method — seismic refraction:
options: P-wave velocity. Depth to bedrock, rippability
questions.solution: Seismic: hammer or explosives. Higher Vp = harder rock. Refraction for shallow (<30m).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3 m seam over 6 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.75. The mineable reserve is about:
questions.solution: Mineable = area×thickness×density×recovery = 6M m²×3×1.4×0.75 ≈ 18.9 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stress (tectonic):
options: σ₁ > σ₂ > σ₃. Maximum, intermediate, minimum principal stresses
questions.solution: Anderson: σ₁ vertical (normal faults), σ₂ vertical (strike-slip), σ₃ vertical (thrust).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using a simplified proxy where calorific value ≈ 70 × fixed-carbon%, a coal with 60% fixed carbon has a calorific value of about:
questions.solution: CV ≈ 70 × 60 = 4200 kcal/kg (illustrative proxy).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lustre in minerals:
options: Metallic (opaque) or non-metallic (vitreous, pearly, silky, earthy)
questions.solution: Metallic: pyrite, galena. Vitreous: quartz. Pearly: talc. Silky: asbestos.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aquifer — specific storage Ss:
options: Volume released per unit volume per unit head change. Elastic (confined). Ss = ρg(α + nβ)
questions.solution: Ss: compressibility of water (β=4.4×10^-10 Pa^-1) and aquifer (α). Confined: S = Ss×b. Unconfined: S_y.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mica group — biotite:
questions.solution: Biotite: black mica. Trioctahedral (3 Mg/Fe per formula unit). Common in many rocks.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Capture zone of a well:
options: Volume of aquifer from which well draws water
questions.solution: Capture zone: area of flow lines terminating at well.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 5 km², is 2 m thick, with density 1.3 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 5×10⁶ m²×2 m×1.3 t/m³ ≈ 13 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 30 m over a horizontal distance of 40 m. Its dip angle is about:
questions.solution: dip = tan⁻¹(rise/run) = tan⁻¹(30/40) ≈ 36.9°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Streak of pyrite:
questions.solution: Pyrite (FeS2) — brassy yellow, greenish-black streak. 'Fool's gold'.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Capillary fringe:
options: Zone above water table where water is held by capillary forces. Saturation > 0
questions.solution: Capillary rise: fine sand 1-2m, silt 2-5m, clay >10m. Negative pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Specific yield S_y:
options: Volume drained by gravity / total volume. Unconfined aquifer storage
questions.solution: S_y: sand 0.2-0.3, clay 0.02-0.05. S_y + S_r = porosity (S_r = specific retention).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentary structures — load cast:
options: Bulbous projections on bottom of bed. Soft sediment deformation
questions.solution: Load cast: differential loading. Flute cast: current scour. Both sole marks (bottom of bed).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Subsidence — mining:
options: Longwall mining → subsidence trough. Angle of draw. Impact on structures
questions.solution: Subsidence: vertical displacement. Tilt and curvature cause damage. Mitigation: backfill, pillars.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cosmogenic nuclide exposure dating (¹⁰Be, ²⁶Al):
options: Nucleides produced by cosmic rays in surface rocks. Higher concentration = longer exposure
questions.solution: ¹⁰Be in quartz. Exposure age = N/(P×(1-e^{-λt})). For surfaces >100ka.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Artificial recharge methods:
options: Check dams, recharge wells, percolation tanks, roof-top harvesting, injection wells
questions.solution: MAR: aquifer storage and recovery (ASR). Water quality treatment. Clogging management.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Permian-Triassic extinction caused by:
options: Siberian Traps volcanism and climate change
questions.solution: P-T extinction: largest in Earth history. ~96% species lost. Caused by Siberian Traps: CO2, SO2, ozone depletion, ocean anoxia.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ore reserve classification (JORC): indicated resource requires:
options: Sufficient exploration for geological confidence but lower than measured
questions.solution: Indicated: enough data for planning. Inferred: limited data. Measured: high confidence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rockfall protection:
options: Barrier, catch ditch, mesh, rock bolt, shotcrete. Warning systems
questions.solution: Rockfall: netting (draped, anchored). Attenuator barriers. Risk: exposure × probability × consequence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eclogite facies:
options: Garnet + omphacite. Very high P. Densest crustal rock (~3.5 g/cm³)
questions.solution: Eclogite: subduction zone. Coesite in UHP rocks. Diamond-grade: >4 GPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pingos:
options: Ice-cored mounds in permafrost. Open-system (groundwater) and closed-system (lake)
questions.solution: Pingo: up to 100m high. Collapse pingo → thermokarst pond. Continuous permafrost zone.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geological Time Scale:
options: Precambrian (88%), Paleozoic (545-251 Ma), Mesozoic (251-66 Ma), Cenozoic (66 Ma-present)
questions.solution: Hadean, Archean, Proterozoic (Precambrian). Phanerozoic: Paleo-Meso-Cenozoic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: (U-Th)/He dating of apatite:
options: He diffusion: closure temperature ~70°C. For exhumation studies
questions.solution: AHe: low-T thermochronometer. (U-Th)/He age = time since cooling through ~70°C.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Ghyben-Herzberg relation describes:
questions.solution: z = 40h. Freshwater head h corresponds to z depth of interface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 50 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: EM spectrum bands used:
options: Visible (0.4-0.7 μm), NIR, SWIR, TIR, Microwave (radar)
questions.solution: Multispectral: multiple bands. Hyperspectral: hundreds of narrow bands.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water table vs potentiometric surface:
options: Water table: phreatic surface (unconfined). Potentiometric: head in confined aquifer
questions.solution: Water table: actual water level in unconfined aquifer. Potentiometric: imaginary surface in confined aquifer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Soil moisture retrieval:
options: Passive microwave (L-band: SMOS, SMAP). Active (C-band: Sentinel-1). Optical/thermal
questions.solution: SMAP: L-band radiometer + radar. 3km, 2-3 day. Soil moisture + freeze/thaw. Surface ~5cm depth.
